name: Zhong Kui
primary_sources: Supplementary Notes to Dream Pool Essays

visual_attributes.morphology: Large man with a big black beard and bulging eyes
distinctive_features: Blue robe with one sleeve stripped
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Robust and imposing figure with a commanding presence

domain_specific_visual.GUARDIAN_ICONOGRAPHY: Often painted on household gates as a guardian spirit

functional_attributes.primary_function: Vanquisher of ghosts and evil beings

domain_specific_functional.EVIL_REPELLING: Used in New-Year Nuo rites to expel evil
domain_specific_functional.FORTUNE_ASSOCIATION: Associated with the five bats of fortune

relationships.description: Travelled with Du Ping to take part in the imperial examinations
Depicted by the Tang painter Wu Daozi based on Emperor Xuanzong's dream
Sworn to rid the world of Xuhao ghosts

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: China, later spread to other East Asian countries
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbol of justice and protection against evil
primary_sources: Supplementary Notes to Dream Pool Essays

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic ghost or demon
NOT a peaceful or serene expression
NOT a modern or Western guardian figure
